authority_boundary: Navigation grouping over the accepted public runtime organ registry. Families are a reader-facing view that buckets organs by what they help you do; they are not a second taxonomy above the architecture kernel, organ registry, standards, or evidence classes.
anti_claim: Family labels, blurbs, and per-family counts are navigation metadata only. They do not encode evidence strength, score-based progress, maturity, readiness, product completeness, release, private-root equivalence, proof correctness, provider-call, secret-export, or whole-system-correctness authority. Evidence strength stays in core/organ_evidence_classes.json; current-authority status stays in core/organ_registry.json.

authority_ceiling.reader_action: Use families to find the cluster of organs relevant to your question, then open the per-organ card in ORGANS.md and the organ's paper module / receipt for evidence. Do not read family membership or family size as maturity, completeness, or release signal.
authority_ceiling.coverage_rule: Every accepted_current_authority organ in core/organ_registry.json maps to exactly one family; every organ_id listed here must exist in the registry. scripts/build_organ_atlas.py fails closed on any gap, extra, or duplicate.

interaction_model.summary: Most organs are standalone replay or validation specimens. They do not call each other; they bind to one shared spine: the architecture kernel primitives (project -> catalog -> pattern -> standard -> route -> work -> event -> evidence -> explanation -> assimilation), the evidence-class registry, and the authority ceiling. A small number are explicitly wired (for example the entry/reveal and import/intake organs feed or consume other named surfaces); each card states standalone vs wired.

families.label: Entry & Reveal
families.blurb: How a cold human or agent first meets Microcosm and what the short guided path actually proves.

families.label: Architecture & Navigation
families.blurb: The kernel primitives, pattern binding, doctrine grammar, route plane, and standards that give the system its shape and let you navigate it.

families.label: Formal Math & Proof
families.blurb: The Lean/proof evidence pipeline: corpus readiness, premise retrieval, tactic routing, verifier trace repair, bounded witnesses, and certificates.

families.label: Agent Reliability & Safety Replays
families.blurb: Source-open replay specimens for agent failure modes: red-team monitors, sabotage, sandbox escape, prompt injection, tool authority, memory poisoning, benchmark gaming, route observability, and provider budgets.

families.label: Research & Science Replays
families.blurb: Replay specimens that project scientific and forecasting workflows: replication rubrics, spatial world models, materials-lab safety, mechanistic interpretability, and prediction reconciliation.

families.label: Import, Projection & Drift
families.blurb: The membrane that brings non-secret macro substrate into the public tree and keeps projections honest instead of letting them drift from source.

families.label: Work, Landing & Continuity
families.blurb: How reversible work transactions are recorded, how dirty-tree landing decisions are made, and how detached runs resume.
